preparation_method: 5 scoops powder dissolved in 200ml water to yield 250ml

serving.description: 1 serving (250ml reconstituted — 5 scoops powder in 200ml water)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hospital serving is 250ml of reconstituted Sustagen Hospital Formula Chocolate, prepared by dissolving 5 scoops of powder into 200ml of water, yielding approximately 260g by weight and providing 290 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Sustagen Hospital Formula Chocolate — Nestlé HealthScience Singapore Product Sheet
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Nestlé HealthScience Singapore product sheet for Sustagen Hospital Formula Chocolate. Per-100g values are derived by scaling from the 250ml reconstituted serving (approximately 260g). This is a USDA Branded Food category equivalent — manufacturer-declared label data. Confidence: HIGH.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of milk-based oral nutritional supplements — analogous estimate based on sucrose/maltodextrin/skim-milk-protein matrix; reference: University of Sydney GI Database (milk-based complete supplement, GI ~52)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Sustagen Hospital Formula Chocolate is published in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~52 is an analogous estimate based on: (1) sucrose + corn maltodextrin carbohydrate base (moderate GI contributors), (2) skim milk protein (whey + casein) moderating postprandial glucose absorption, and (3) comparable complete milk-based oral nutritional supplements in the University of Sydney GI Database with GI values in the range 40–55. Confidence: 0.9. GL per 250ml serving = GI × available carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 52 × 44 / 100 = 22.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (84.6mg/100g; 220mg per 250ml serving) with favourable potassium level (142.3mg/100g; 370mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Sustagen Hospital Formula Chocolate is appropriate for hypertensive patients; at two servings per day the formula contributes only 440mg sodium, preserving adequate daily sodium budget, while the potassium content supports blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~52 (low), GL ~23 per serving; sucrose and maltodextrin carbohydrate base is not optimised for glycaemic control compared with diabetes-specific formulas (e.g. Glucerna Triple Care, GI ~28)
type2_diabetes.guidance: For patients with Type 2 Diabetes, Glucerna Triple Care is strongly preferred over Sustagen Hospital Formula; if Sustagen is used due to cost or palatability constraints, blood glucose monitoring is required and insulin or oral hypoglycaemic medication adjustment may be necessary.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.8g/100g; 2g per serving), zero trans fat, low cholesterol (5mg/100g), and low sodium; complete micronutrient fortification including vitamin E (2.2mg/100g) and selenium (9mcg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Sustagen Hospital Formula Chocolate has a favourable cardiovascular profile with low saturated fat, zero trans fat, and low sodium, making it appropriate as an oral nutritional supplement for patients with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High calcium content (134.6mg/100g; 350mg per serving, approximately 35% RDA) and complete dairy protein (14g per serving)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The high calcium content is clinically significant for rheumatoid arthritis patients on long-term corticosteroids who are at risk of corticosteroid-induced osteoporosis; Sustagen Hospital Formula is appropriate for this population.

malnutrition.key_factor: SFA-registered FSMP for general disease-related malnutrition; 290 kcal and 14g protein per 250ml serving; complete micronutrient profile
malnutrition.guidance: Sustagen Hospital Formula Chocolate is the standard first-line oral nutritional supplement in Singapore public hospital wards for patients with general disease-related malnutrition, providing a cost-effective, nutritionally complete option.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sustagen Hospital Formula Chocolate is a powder-format oral nutritional supplement (ONS) manufactured by Nestlé HealthScience and registered in Singapore as a Food for Special Medical Purposes (FSMP) under the Singapore Food Agency (SFA). It is the standard first-line hospital oral supplement in Singapore public hospital wards, selected for its cost advantage over Ensure Plus while providing a nutritionally complete profile suitable for patients with general disease-related malnutrition. Preparation method: each serving is made by dissolving 5 scoops of powder into 200ml of cool or warm water to yield 250ml of smooth, chocolate-flavoured reconstituted liquid. The texture is similar to a thin chocolate milk drink — mild, sweet, and palatable for hospital patients who may have reduced appetite or swallowing difficulties. Each 250ml serving provides approximately 290 kcal, 14g protein from a dairy whey and casein blend, 7g fat (2g saturated fat, zero trans fat), 44g carbohydrate (20g sugar from sucrose and maltodextrin), 220mg sodium, 370mg potassium, and 350mg calcium. The formula is comprehensively fortified with vitamins A, C, D, E, B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, folate, iron, zinc, selenium, magnesium, and phosphorus, making it nutritionally complete for use as a sole or supplementary source of nutrition. Glycaemic index is approximately 52 (low GI category), with a glycaemic load of approximately 23 per serving, reflecting the sucrose and maltodextrin carbohydrate base moderated by the milk protein matrix. It is halal-certified, vegetarian-suitable, and gluten-free. Clinically, it is NOT recommended as the primary supplement for Type 2 Diabetes patients, for whom Glucerna Triple Care (GI ~28) is strongly preferred due to its optimised glycaemic response. Sustagen Hospital Formula is appropriate for patients with hypertension (low sodium), cardiovascular disease (low saturated fat, zero trans fat), rheumatoid arthritis (high calcium for corticosteroid-induced osteoporosis prevention), and general malnutrition. It is available in all Singapore public hospital wards and is also used for home nutrition support post-discharge.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Nestlé HealthScience Singapore product sheet (manufacturer-declared label data) with confidence 1.0 for all core macros and micronutrients. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence 0.9) based on comparable milk-based complete supplements in the University of Sydney GI Database. Overall record confidence is HIGH.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not reported on the Nestlé HealthScience Singapore product sheet for Sustagen Hospital Formula Chocolate and is not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific product as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content of the reconstituted formula is not explicitly reported by the manufacturer and is not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific product as of 2026-04-14. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per Nestlé HealthScience Singapore product sheet. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per Nestlé HealthScience Singapore product sheet. GI ~52 is an analogous estimate; no direct published GI measurement for Sustagen Hospital Formula Chocolate exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04-14. This product is NOT recommended as the primary oral supplement for Type 2 Diabetes patients; Glucerna Triple Care is strongly preferred. Per-100g values are derived by scaling from the 250ml reconstituted serving (approximately 260g total weight). Serving size of 260g is estimated based on 5 scoops powder (~60g) dissolved in 200ml water (200g), yielding approximately 260g reconstituted product.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Populated local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for Singaporean context.
Iteration 2: Added newly populated local names to flat aliases array and aliases_by_language to maintain consistency and improve multilingual synonym coverage.

Iteration 3: Substantially expanded natural_language_description to explicitly cover preparation method, sensory profile (texture, flavour, palatability), clinical context, regulatory status, key nutrients, GI interpretation, dietary flags, and specific clinical recommendations for all relevant conditions — resolving q6_llm_ready_semantics failure from iteration 2 validator feedback.
